title: glandularia tomophylla
intro: Glandularia tomophylla, also known as sticky geranium, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and sticky leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid. The health benefits of Glandularia tomophylla include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Glandularia tomophylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical use, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing and soothe sore muscles.
